description: Parallel chunk PUT + GET on the RADOS data backend. Closes two open ROADMAP P2 entries: 'Parallel chunk upload in PutChunks' + 'Parallel chunk read / prefetch in GetChunks'. Today `internal/data/rados/backend.go::PutChunks` writes 4 MiB chunks SEQUENTIALLY in a `for { io.ReadFull; writeChunk; loop }` shape â€” RADOS round-trip latency stacks linearly with object size. `GetChunks` returns a `radosReader` that fetches the next chunk only when the current one is drained â€” same sequential RADOS-latency-stacking on streaming reads. The S3 data backend is unaffected (AWS SDK `manager.Uploader` already parallelises internally); memory backend is tests-only. Fix scope: bounded worker pool (default 32, configurable via `STRATA_RADOS_PUT_CONCURRENCY` / `STRATA_RADOS_GET_PREFETCH`) with order-preserving manifest assembly on PUT and memory-bounded prefetch on GET (cancel-on-client-cancel). Multi-cluster manifests (US-044 shipped the cluster fan-out) automatically benefit since chunk-write dispatch is cluster-agnostic â€” the worker pool dials the right ioctx per chunk via the existing `resolveClass` / `ioctx` helpers. 3 stories. Closes ROADMAP P2 'Parallel chunk upload in `PutChunks`' + P2 'Parallel chunk read / prefetch in `GetChunks`' on cycle close.

userStories.title: Parallel PutChunks worker pool (RADOS backend)
userStories.description: As an S3 client uploading a multi-chunk object, I want the gateway to dispatch RADOS chunk writes concurrently so PUT latency scales sub-linearly with object size â€” hiding per-chunk OSD round-trip latency.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/data/rados/backend.go::PutChunks` no longer writes chunks sequentially. Bounded worker pool (default concurrency 32) reads chunks from the input stream in order, dispatches `writeChunk` to N workers, and assembles `data.Manifest.Chunks` in submission order â€” manifest chunk order MUST equal source byte order regardless of completion order
Concurrency configurable via `STRATA_RADOS_PUT_CONCURRENCY` env (range [1, 256], default 32, validation at Backend construction time)
On first error from any worker, cancel in-flight workers, drain the pool, and call `cleanupManifest` over the chunks that DID land in RADOS â€” no leaked partial-write OIDs
Reader cancel (`ctx.Err()`) is honoured at chunk-read boundary AND at worker dispatch (don't block on a backed-up pool when the client has given up)

ETag computation (running MD5 over all chunk bytes in source order) is preserved â€” must equal the byte-stream's MD5, not a per-chunk-completion-order hash; achieve via a single goroutine that owns the hasher + reads from the source stream in order, dispatches `(idx, []byte)` work units to the pool, and waits for `(idx, err)` results to land before sealing the manifest
Observer (`ObserveOp`) still fires per chunk write with correct duration; tracer spans (`data.rados.put`) still emit one span per OID

New unit test (`internal/data/rados/backend_test.go` or a fresh `backend_parallel_test.go`): drive PutChunks against a fake `ioctx` that injects ~5 ms latency per write; assert wall-clock for a 32-chunk object is well below 32Ă— single-chunk latency (e.g. â‰¤ 4Ă— single-chunk on concurrency=8). Use a deterministic mock, not a sleep-and-pray timing race
Race detector (`go test -race ./internal/data/rados/...`) clean against the new code
Existing tests in `internal/data/rados/` still pass â€” round-trip behaviour, ETag, cleanup on error, multi-cluster ioctx resolution unchanged
`internal/data/rados/observer_test.go` parity: per-chunk observability is preserved
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented in internal/data/rados/parallel.go (putChunksParallel + putConcurrencyFromEnv) and wired into internal/data/rados/backend.go::PutChunks. Concurrency knob STRATA_RADOS_PUT_CONCURRENCY [1,256] default 32 read at Backend constructor time. Unit tests in internal/data/rados/parallel_test.go (no build tag, ceph-free) cover source-byte-order preservation, ETag-MD5 invariant, parallel wall-clock claim, error-cancel + cleanup, reader cancel, completion-out-of-order ordering, env clamping. Race-detector clean.

userStories.title: Prefetch chunk N+1 in GetChunks (RADOS backend)
userStories.description: As an S3 client streaming a large GET, I want the gateway to fetch the next chunk from RADOS while the current one is draining to the wire â€” so streaming throughput is bounded by network egress, not by per-chunk OSD fetch latency.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/data/rados/backend.go::GetChunks` returns a reader that prefetches the NEXT chunk while the caller drains the CURRENT one. Default prefetch depth = 4 (16 MiB inflight); configurable via `STRATA_RADOS_GET_PREFETCH` env (range [1, 64])
Prefetch is memory-bounded: at most `prefetch_depth * chunk_size` bytes inflight (default 4 Ă— 4 MiB = 16 MiB). No unbounded buffering on slow-client / fast-RADOS

Prefetch is abortable: when `radosReader.Close` fires (or `ctx.Done`), in-flight prefetches are cancelled and goroutines exit cleanly within a bounded window (no goroutine leak detectable by `go test -race -run TestGetChunksClientCancel`)

Read order is preserved â€” the reader emits bytes in exact chunk-order regardless of fetch completion order. The prefetch pool reads chunk[i+1..i+N] in parallel but the reader's Read() pulls them off an ordered channel/queue
Range GET (offset > 0 or length < manifest.Size) still works correctly â€” first chunk fetched is the one containing `offset`, the prefetch window stops at the chunk containing `offset+length`
Observer + tracer continue to fire per chunk fetch â€” duration reflects the actual OSD `Read` time, not wait-on-channel time

New unit test (`internal/data/rados/backend_test.go` or `backend_prefetch_test.go`): drive GetChunks against a fake `ioctx` with per-chunk Read latency; assert wall-clock for a 32-chunk full-read is well below 32Ă— single-chunk latency (e.g. â‰¤ 8Ă— on prefetch_depth=4)
New unit test for client-cancel: Close the reader mid-stream, assert all prefetch goroutines exit within 500 ms (use `goleak` or count-running-goroutines pattern)
Race detector (`go test -race ./internal/data/rados/...`) clean
Existing tests in `internal/data/rados/` still pass â€” sequential range-GET correctness, EOF on short manifest, multi-cluster ioctx resolution unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Benchmarks + docs + ROADMAP close-flip
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a recorded throughput baseline + operator-facing tuning doc + ROADMAP close-flip for both P2 entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Go benchmark `internal/data/rados/backend_bench_test.go::BenchmarkPutChunks` + `BenchmarkGetChunks` driving 64 MiB / 256 MiB objects against a fake ioctx with realistic ~5 ms per-op latency; benchmark output recorded in `docs/site/content/architecture/benchmarks/parallel-chunks.md` (or appended to existing `meta-backend-comparison.md` style page)

Doc page covers: (a) what changed (PUT + GET parallelism), (b) tuning knobs (`STRATA_RADOS_PUT_CONCURRENCY` default 32, `STRATA_RADOS_GET_PREFETCH` default 4), (c) memory budget per request (concurrency Ă— chunk_size, prefetch_depth Ă— chunk_size), (d) when to raise / lower (small-object workloads benefit from lower concurrency to reduce overhead; large-object workloads from higher), (e) cross-cluster manifest behaviour (multi-cluster fan-out still works; per-cluster ioctx resolution stays in the worker)
Reference doc table (`docs/site/content/reference/_index.md`) gains an env-var entry pair for the two new knobs

`scripts/s3-tests/run.sh` against `make up-all` local stack confirms no regression on the existing baseline (162/177 â†’ 165/178 from prior cycle; this cycle should not change pass rate either way â€” verify)

ROADMAP.md close-flip in the same commit per CLAUDE.md Roadmap maintenance rule: BOTH P2 entries ('Parallel chunk upload in `PutChunks`' + 'Parallel chunk read / prefetch in `GetChunks`') flip to `~~**P2 â€” ...**~~ â€” **Done.** ... (commit `<pending>`)`
Closing-SHA backfill follow-up commit on main per established pattern (commit-then-amend is undesirable)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
